rationale: X's core claim — that both submit_initial and submit_update reject list-shaped diffusion prompts, so gating the unwrap on already_submitted was unnecessary — is nearly verbatim the reasoning yJader gave in the sole inline comment, and X correctly lands on APPROVE matching the actual outcome (2x LGTM, merged). Y reaches the same line but justifies it via a different, weaker argument (_prewarm_async_chunk_stages scalar-passing) that misses yJader's specific 'both paths reject lists' insight, and Y inflates missing-test gaps to three 'major' REQUEST CHANGES items despite two human approvals with no test ask — a calibration miss that hurts precision even though the technical evidence is grounded. Y is more verbose/actionable on hypothetical test additions, but X is better matched to what the ground-truth reviewers actually flagged and concluded.
